name: VideoVault Premiere Extension
releaseNotes:
- New dark editorial shell for the Premiere extension.
- Clearer `Overview`, `Proxy Manager`, and `Comments` navigation.
- Proxy verification now surfaces missing-server, NAS, and local-cache counts more clearly.
- Comments now have an in-extension scaffold so the next implementation can connect real data without another UI rewrite.
- Redesigned the panel shell to feel closer to modern creative extensions rather than a utility dialog.
- Added a top-level hero/status area that explains the current workflow focus.
- Added a primary navigation layer for:
- `Overview`
- `Proxy Manager`
- `Comments`
- Kept the existing proxy/cache state machine intact while presenting it in a cleaner, more production-ready layout.
- Surfaced proxy verification outcomes in a new summary strip:
- clips missing proxies on the server
- clips available on VideoVault Server
- clips already in the local cache
- Preserved the existing `PROXY_NEEDED` bridge path so missing-proxy detection can still escalate to VideoVault Connect.
- Added an in-extension comments scaffold page to prepare for the upcoming read-only comments feature and later reply/resolve flows.
- Extension version bumped to `1.7.0`.
- Update `extensions/adobe-premiere/latest.json` in `videovault-release-updates` after packaging the new macOS and Windows installers.
- Release tag should follow:
- `premiere-extension-v1.7.0`
